titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Alfred A. Hart and Carleton E. Watkins Stereographs of the Central Pacific Railroad
(Hart) Alfred A. and Carleton E. Watkins Stereographs of the Central Pacific Railroad
titlestmt.author: Sarah Lerner

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0, has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 15768340.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday January 9, 2025 at 00:34

did.unittitle: Alfred A. Hart and Carleton E. Watkins Stereographs of the Central Pacific Railroad

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Albumen stereographs and half stereographs published by Alfred A. Hart and Carleton E. Watkins of the construction of the Central Pacific Railroad in California and Utah, 1865-1869.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: From the collection of Harry C. Peterson. Purchased from Andrew Smith Gallery Inc. on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged by number appearing in title on mount.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Alfred A. Hart (1816-1906) was the official photographer of the Central Pacific Railroad in the 1860s. Hart eventually sold his negatives to photographer Carleton E. Watkins (1829-1916), who then continued to publish Hart's stereoviews of the railroad's construction.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Alfred A. Hart and Carleton E. Watkins Stereographs of the Central Pacific Railroad. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.
Item-level listing was pulled from a dealer-provided inventory. File titles have been transcribed from stereograph mounts.

This finding aid was revised in 2023 to add this processing note to contextualize a racist and sexist descriptor used in the folder title for item 282, which contains a racist and sexist descriptor transcribed from a stereograph mount. The use of this title is not an endorsement of the language it contains. Original descriptive language has been retained to promote searchability and discoverability. Please contact the Beinecke Library for details or if you have questions or comments about these revisions. For more information on reparative archival description at Yale, see Yale's Statement on Harmful Language in Archival Description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Albumen stereographs and half stereographs published by Alfred A. Hart and Carleton E. Watkins of the construction of the Central Pacific Railroad in California and Utah, 1865-1869.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Alfred A. Hart and Carleton E. Watkins Stereographs of the Central Pacific Railroad is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: 001. [Locomotive Stanford. Probably at Newcastle.]
003. Depot and Trestle, at Newcastle.
004. Road above Newcastle, Placer County.
005. View in Dutch Ravine, Placer County.
006. View in Dutch Ravine, 32 miles from Sacramento.
007. Embankment in Dutch Ravine, above Newcastle.
008. [Approaching Bloomer Cut.]
009. Bloomer Cut, 800 feet long, looking East.
010. Bloomer Cut, birdseye view, 63 feet deep, 800 long.
011. Bloomer Cut and Embankment, looking East.
012. Bloomer Cut, 63 feet high, looking West.
013. View in Bloomer Cut, near Auburn.
013b. View in Bloomer Cut, Near Auburn.
015. High Bank, Baltimore Ravine.
016. Rock Cut, near Auburn, Placer County.
017. Rock Ravine, near Auburn.
018. High Embankment, near Auburn.
019. [Trestle, opposite Auburn.]
021. Auburn Depot, Altitude 1,385 feet, 36 miles from Sacramento.
022. Road East Of Station, at Auburn.
023. Road In Auburn Ravine, Placer County.
024. Lime Point, above Auburn.
025. High Embankment, Auburn Ravine.
027. Trestle near Lovell's Ranch, 40 miles from Sacramento.
028. Road and Trestle, near Lovell's Ranch.
029. Trestle in Clipper Ravine, near Clipper Gap.
030. Trestle Bridge, 120 feet high, 600 feet long, Clipper Ravine.
031. Trestle Bridge, Clipper Ravine, near view.
034. Locomotive Atlantic at Colfax, Placer County.
035. Depot At Colfax, 500 feet long. 55 miles from Sacramento.
036. Colfax from the South, Altitude 2,448 feet.
037. Teamster's Camp, at Colfax.
038. Cañon of American River from near Colfax, Cape Horn and Railroad on the left.
039. Long Ravine Bridge, from the top of Cape Horn.
040. Long Ravine Bridge from the West. 56 miles from Sacramento.
041. Long Ravine Bridge, near Colfax. Length 1,050 Feet.
042. Long Ravine Bridge from below. 120 feet high.
043. Cape Horn and Railroad from the west. Hight [sic] above ravine 1,400 Feet.
044. Amer. R. and Canyon, from Cape Horn--river below R.R.1,400 feet.
044a. Am. River and Cañon from Cape Horn, River below Railroad 1,400 feet. 57 miles from Sacramento.
044b. Am. River and Cañon from Cape Horn, River below railroad 1,400 feet. 57 miles from Sacramento.
045. Sawmill and Cut east Of Cape Horn. 59 miles.
047. Secret Town. 62 miles from Sacramento. Altitude 3,000 feet.
048. Secrettown Trestle from the West. Length 1,100 feet.
049. Secrettown Trestle from the East.
050. Tunnel Hill Cut, Depth 111 feet. 63 miles from Sacramento.
051. Bear River Valley--You Bet and mines in the distance.
052. Bear River Valley, near Gold Run, Little York mines in the distance.
053. Cut through "Dixie Spur," 64 miles from Sacramento.
054. Gold Run and Railroad Cut. Altitude 3,245 feet.
055. Flume and Railroad at Gold Run.
056. Rounding Cape Horn--
057. Excursion Train At Cape Horn.
058. Secret Ravine, Iowa Hill in the distance. 61 miles from Sacramento.
059. Hornet Hill Cut--west of Gold Run. 50 feet deep.
060. Train in Dixie Cut. Gold Run Station, Placer County.
061. Hydraulic Mining, at Gold Run, Placer County.
061a. Hydraulic Mining, at Gold Run, Placer County.
062. Embankment below Dutch Flat, Placer County.
063. Dutch Flat, Placer County, 67 miles from Sacramento.
063a. Dutch Flat, Placer County, 67 Miles.
064. [Dutch Flat.]
065. View near Dutch Flat.
066. Sandstone Cut, near Alta, Placer County.
067. Alta from the South. Altitude 3,635 feet. 69 miles from Sacramento.
068. Alta from the North. Altitude 3,040 feet. 69 miles from Sacramento.
069. The Huntington at Alta, Placer County.
070. Blasting at Chalk Bluffs above Alta. Cut 60 feet deep.
071. Building Bank across Cañon Creek. 87 feet high.
072. Culvert at Canon Creek. 185 feet long--12 feet span.
073. Cut above Alta, Placer County.
074. Secrettown Bridge. 1,100 feet long. 62 miles from Sacramento.
075. Superintendent Strobridge and Family, at Alta.
076. Giant's Gap, American River. 2,500 feet perpendicular. 72 miles from Sacrament.
077. Green Valley and Giant's Gap, American River, 1,500 feet below Railroad.
078. Green Bluffs. 1,500 feet above American River. 71 miles from Sac.
079. View West of Prospect Hill. 75 miles from Sacramento.
080. Prospect Hill from Camp 21. 75 miles from Sacramento.
081. Little Blue Cañon, 74 miles from Sacramento.
082. Prospect Hill Cut, Upper slope, 170 feet.
083. Prospect Hill Cut, from the North.
084. View at China Ranch. 75 miles from Sacramento.
085. Fort Point Cut, 70 feet deep, 600 feet long.
086. View North of Fort Point. 76 miles from Sacramento.
087. Horse Ravine. 77 miles.
088. Horse Ravine Wall and Grizzly Hill Tunnel. 77 miles from Sacramento.
089. Grizzly Hill Tunnel from the North. 500 feet long.
090. Bank and Cut at Sailor's Spur, 80 miles from Sacramento.
091. Owl Gap Cut. 900 feet long, 45 feet deep. 80 miles from Sacramento.
092. Heath's Ravine Bank. 82 feet high. 82 miles from Sacramento.
093. Black Butte and Crystal Lake. 90 miles from Sacramento.
094. Crystal Lake, Altitude 5,907 feet. 90 miles from Sacramento.
095. Crystal Lake. Altitude 5,907 feet.
096. Cascades on the Yuba River, near Crystal Lake.
097. Rattlesnake Mountain and Cascades, on Yuba River, near Cisco.
098. Black Butte, from the North.
099. Lower Cisco, Placer County, 92 miles from Sacramento.
100. Yuba Cascade and Hieroglyphic Rocks, on the Yuba River, near Crystal Lake.

102. Hieroglyphic Rocks, on the Yuba River, near Crystal Lake.
103. Hieroglyphic Rocks, on the Yuba River, near Crystal Lake.

105. New Hampshire Rocks, on Yuba River. Summer ciew. 96 miles from Sacramento.
106. New Hampshire Falls, on Yuba River. Summer view, 96 miles from Sacramento.
107. New Hampshire Rocks, looking down the River.
108. Scene on Yuba River, above Cisco.
109. Summit Valley, Altitude 6,960 feet. Emigrant Mt. and R.R. Pass in dist.
110. Castle Peak from Lava Bluff, 10,000 feet above the sea. Western Summit.
111. Castle Peak And Yuba River, from Summit Valley, 102 miles from Sacramento.
112. Scene near Donner Pass. Table Peak in the distance.
113. Castle Peak from Grant's Butte, Western Summit.
114. Scene at Lake Angela. Altitude 7,300 feet.
115. Lake Angela, Mt. King In The Distance. Western Summit.
116. Camp near Summit Tunnel, Mount King in the distance.
117. Bluffs in Donner Pass, Western Summit, 500 feet high. Altitude of Pass 7,090 feet.
118. Summit Tunnel--Eastern Portal. Length 1,000 feet, on the Western Summit.
119. Laborers and Rocks, near opening of Summit Tunnel.
120. Scene near Summit Tunnel, Eastern slope of Western Summit.
121. Grant's Peak and Palisade Rocks, from Western Summit.
122. Palisade Rocks with Road and Teams descending Western Summit.
123. Lakeview Bluff, 350 feet high, from the Wagon Road.
124. Road and Rocks at foot of Crested Peak, Eastern slope of Western Summit.
125. Donner Lake from Summit. Lakeview Bluff on the right.
126. Donner Lake and Eastern Summit, from top of Summit Tunnel, Western Summit.
127. Donner Lake, Eastern Summits 25 miles distant.
128. Boating Party on Donner Lake.
128a. [Boating Party Donner Lake].
129. Donner Lake, with Crested Peak and Mt. Lincoln in distance.
130. View on Donner Lake. Altitude 5,964 feet.
131. Donner Lake, with pass in distance, Altitude above lake, 1,126 feet.
132. Donner Lake, Peak and Pass, from Wagon Road.
133. Stumps cut by Donner Party in 1846.
134. Dry Creek Bridge, 17 miles from Sacramento.
135. Locomotive on Trestle, near American River.
136. Train and Curve, Jenny Lind Flat.
137. Bound for the Mountains, 12 mile Tangent--4 miles from Sacramento.
138. Freight Depot at Newcastle. Placer County, 31 miles from Sacramento.
139. Locomotive, on Turntable.
140. Rocklin Granite Quarry, 22 miles from Sacramento.
141. Tangent below Pino, 23 miles from Sacramento.

149. Colfax, looking West -Illinoistown in dist.
150. Colfax, looking East, Cape Horn 4 miles and Giant's Gap 20 miles distant.
151. Cape Horn, from Ravine below.
151a. Cape Horn from Ravine below.
153. Hog's Back Cut, 60 feet deep. 2 miles above Alta.
154. American River, from Green Bluffs.
155. View at the Forks of the American River.
156. Prospect Hill Cut, 150 Feet Deep, 74 Feet Wide.

162. Lost Camp Spur Cut, 80 miles from Sacramento.
163. Frame for Snow Covering, interior view.
164. Emigrant Gap, Snow Plow And Turntable.
165. Emigrant Gap, West from Tunnel.

167. Emigrant Gap, looking East, Yuba Mountains in distance.
168. Bear Valley, 85 miles from Sacramento.
169. Valley North Fork of Yuba, above Emigrant Gap, Old Man Mountain.
170. Cement Ridge--Old Man Mountain in dist.
171. Miller's Bluffs, near Crystal Lake, Old Man Mountain in distance.
172. Echo Point, opposite Crystal Lake, looking West.
173. Echo Point and Rattlesnake Mountains.
174. Railroad, below Cisco And Crystal Lake
175. Foot of Black Butte, above Crystal Lake.
176. Black Butte, 91 miles from Sacramento.
177. Crystal Lake and Railroad, from Black Butte.
178. South Yuba Valley And Summit, from Black Butte.
179. Old Man Mountain, Altitude 7,500.
180. Meadow Lake--6,800 Feet Elevation. Knickerbocker Hill and Old Man Mountain.

185. Depots at Cisco.
186. View of the South Yuba, below Cisco.
187. Summits of Sierras. 8,000 to 10,000 feet altitude.

190. Summit of Castle Peak, from the North-west.
191. Summit Valley, from Emigrant Mount. Alt. 8,200 feet, looking toward Cisco.

218. Tunnel No. 3, above Cisco.

230. View on the American River, below Cape Horn.
230. View on the American river, below Cape Horn.
231. Bloomer Cut near Auburn, 800 Feet Long And 63 Feet High.

236. Cathedral Rocks, Truckee River.
237. Crested Peak, From Grant's Butte.
238. Cloud View, Donner Lake.
239. Snow Plow.
240. Engine House and Train. Rocklin, 22 miles from Sacramento.
241. Engine House and Turntable. Rocklin, 22 miles from Sacramento.
242. West of Clipper Gap. Placer County.
243. Clipper Gap, 43 miles from Sacramento.
244. Cut near New England Mills. 43 miles from Sacramento.
245. Cape Horn, from Ravine below.
246. Constructing Snow Cover. Scene near the Summit.
246a. Constructing Snow Cover. Scene near the Summit.
247. Frame of Snow Covering. 90 miles from Sacramento.
248. Lower Cascade. Near Long Slide Track.
249. Lower Cascade Bridge. Above Cisco.
250. Upper Cascade, 98 miles from Sacramento.
251. Upper Cascade Bridge. Above Cisco.
252. Snow Gallery around Crested Peak. Timbers 12x14 in., 20 in. apart.
253. Crested Peak, from Railroad. Roof of Snow Gallery.
254. Inside view of Snow Gallery at Summit. Bolting the Frame to the Rocks.
255. From Tunnel No. 10 looking West. Building Great Wall across the Ravine.
256. Crested Peak and Tunnel No. 10. Eastern Slope of Western Summit.
257. Tunnel No. 12. Strong's Canyon.
258. Castle Peak, from Railroad. Above Donner Lake.
259. Coldstream Valley, East of Donner Lake.
260. Mist rising from Donner Lake. Early Morning View.
261. Railroad around Crested Peak. View from foot of Donner Lake.
262. Depot at Truckee. 119 miles from Sacramento.
262(a). Depot at Truckee. 119 miles from Sacramento.
263. Scene at Truckee. Nevada County.
264. Truckee River, at Truckee Station. 15 miles from Lake Tahoe.
265. Boca. Crossing of Little Truckee.
266. View of Truckee River. Near Camp 24.
267. View near the State Line. Truckee River.
268. Boundary Peak and Tunnel No. 15. 137 miles from Sacramento.
269. Tunnel No. 15. Looking East, toward Nevada.
270. Tunnel No. 15, Near Camp 24.
271. Bridge near State Line. 138 miles from Sacramento.
272. Second Crossing of Truckee River, near Camp 24.
273. Bridge at Eagle Gap, Truckee River.
274. Bridge over Truckee River. Eagle Gap.
275. Eagle Gap. Truckee River.
276. View Near Verdi, Truckee River.
277. Looking toward Verdi. Truckee River, 140 miles from Sacramento.
279 Fourth Crossing of Truckee River. 147 miles from Sacramento.
279a. Forth Crossing Of Truckee River, 147 Miles From Sacramento
280. Granite Quarry, near Reno.
281. Reno and Washoe Range in distance. From Base of Sierra Nevada Mountains.
282. Piute Squaws And Children.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This folder title contains racist and sexist language in its description of Paiute (Piute) people, and the use of this title is not an endorsement of the language it contains. For more information, see the collection level processing note.

did.unittitle: 283. Piute Indians. at Reno.

288. Looking across Truckee Meadows, toward Sierra Nevada Mountains, near Camp 37.

290. Truckee Meadows, from Camp 37. 162 miles from Sacramento.

292. Below Camp 37, Lower Canyon of Truckee.

297. View from Basaltic Rocks. Looking East.

308. Wadsworth, Big Bend of Truckee River, Washoe Range in distance.

312. Alkali Flat, Construction Train in distance.

324. Shoshone Indians, Humboldt Plains.

327. Chinese Camp. At end of Track.

330. Commencement of a Snow Storm, Scene East of Second Crossing of Humboldt.

332. Team Camp--Evening View, End of Track.

334. Humboldt Gate. Ten Mile Canyon.

342. Shoshone Indians, Ten Mile Cañon.

347. Elko from the West. 468 miles from Sacramento.
348. Water Tank at Peko. 488 miles from Sacramento.
349(a). [Loco Leviathan Near Deeth, Mt. Halleck In Distance.]
349. Scene near Deeth, Mount Halleck in distance.
350. Railroad Camp near Victory. 10 1/4 miles laid in one day.
351. Monument Point from the lake, 669 miles from Sacramento.
352. Salt Lake from Monument Point. 669 miles from Sacramento.
353. Poetry and Prose, Scene at Monument Point, north end of Salt Lake.
354. The First Greeting of the Iron Horse. Promontory Point, May 9th, 1869.
355. The Last Rail--The Invocation. Fixing the Wire, May 10th, 1869.
356. The Last Act--690 miles from Sacramento. Scene at Promontory Point, May 10th 1869.

360. The Last Act--690 miles from Sacramento. Scene at Promontory Point, May 10th, 1869.
361. Looking West from Taylor's Mills, near Ogden.
363. Ogden and Wasatch Range, 742 miles from Sacramento.
364. Railroad at Ogden, Wasatch Range in distance.
